nfcu_wf_greeting.ai_message: Good morning, Janelle. I have flagged 3 quality signals. The most urgent: BSA/AML verification is being skipped in 23% of new account calls — a compliance risk that started 5 days ago and correlates with a recent script change. Want me to walk you through them?
suggested_chips: Walk me through the compliance issue
Show me the repeat contact data

confidence.rationale: All three signals are direct telemetry; the script-change correlation is the only inference and it has a 5-day lookback alignment.

nfcu_wf_signal_1_compliance.ai_message: This is a NCUA compliance exposure — not an agent behavior problem. The script change created a structural failure point: by moving verification to step 7, agents are completing calls before they reach it. The immediate fix is a script rollback to put verification back at step 3. Of the 142 affected accounts, 134 can be auto-remediated; 8 need direct callbacks. I can generate the remediation list now.
suggested_chips: Walk me through the compliance issue

confidence.rationale: Process-adherence telemetry directly logs each step. Account count and segmentation between auto-remediable vs. callback are deterministic CRM joins.

nfcu_wf_signal_2_repeat_contact.ai_message: Coaching agents won't fix this — the problem is upstream. IT has a known sync latency issue (logged April 14, fix April 25) and agents are being asked to paper over it with workarounds that fail. The right intervention is a holding statement script so members know what's happening and when it will be resolved, rather than triggering a second call. FCR will stay depressed until April 25 regardless of quality coaching.
suggested_chips: Show me the repeat contact data

confidence.rationale: IT incident → repeat-contact correlation is strong (Pearson 0.87 on 9 days of data). Workaround failure rate of 78% is direct telemetry.

nfcu_wf_signal_3_burnout.ai_message: Losing any one of these three would materially hurt mortgage servicing quality — they carry your most complex calls and replacing them takes 4–6 months at minimum. The critical insight: this pattern typically shows 60–90 days before they resign. You're inside that window now. A structured intervention — workload rebalancing, a recognition moment, or a direct check-in — has a 68% documented retention rate at this stage. I can flag them for a manager outreach task in Dynamics 365.
suggested_chips: Show me the repeat contact data

confidence.rationale: Burnout pattern model has 0.79 precision against 24 prior cases; 68% retention figure is a documented intervention outcome with ±9% variance.

**(2) Generate the remediation list** for the 142 affected accounts:
• 134 accounts: CRM records have sufficient identity data — auto-resolvable without member contact
• 8 accounts: member callback required — I have drafted outreach scripts for each case

Should I execute both actions?

nfcu_wf_turn_3_repeat_contacts.ai_message:
Bill pay repeat contacts are up 31% this week. I correlated Dynamics 365 call transcripts with the IT incident log — something Dynamics 365 cannot do alone:

**Root cause:** A digital banking sync latency issue logged by IT on April 14 causes payment confirmations to appear delayed. Members don't see the payment, call in. Agents provide a manual workaround — 'wait 4 hours and check again.' The workaround fails for 78% of callers, who call back.

**Impact:**
• First-call resolution for bill pay: 81% → **54%**
• Repeat contact rate (48hr): up 31%
• Agent time absorbed by repeat calls: estimated 180 additional agent-hours this week

**IT fix ETA:** April 25. Until then, the workaround is the only option — but agents can be coached to set more accurate expectations and reduce repeat calls by 40–50%.
suggested_chips: When is the IT fix expected?
How much is this costing us?

confidence.rationale: FCR drop and repeat rate are direct reads; 40–50% reduction estimate from coaching is a 5-event historical band.

nfcu_wf_agent_coaching.ai_message:
For bill pay calls until the April 25 IT fix:

**Recommended agent script update:**
'We are aware of a payment display delay affecting some accounts. Your payment has processed — it will appear in your balance within 2–4 hours due to a current system update. If it does not appear by tomorrow morning, please call us back and we will resolve it immediately.'

This script:
• Sets accurate expectations (not just 'wait and check')
• Reduces callbacks from 78% to a projected 35–40%
• Avoids member frustration from vague workarounds

I can push this as a script note to all bill pay queue agents in Dynamics 365 now.

nfcu_wf_cost_impact.ai_message:
Here's the cost picture for the bill pay repeat contact spike:

**Agent Time Impact:**
• 180 additional agent-hours absorbed this week by repeat bill pay calls
• At an average fully-loaded cost of $28/hour: **$5,040 in wasted agent capacity this week alone**
• Projected to week 2 (before April 25 IT fix): another ~$4,500

**Member Experience Cost:**
• 47 member complaints logged MTD — bill pay is the top driver
• Repeat callers have a 23% higher attrition rate in the 90 days following a failed resolution
• Estimated member retention risk: ~340 impacted members × 23% = **78 members at elevated churn risk**

**Avoided Cost (with coaching fix):**
• Improved agent scripting (already deployed) is projected to reduce callbacks by 40–50%
• That recovers ~72–90 agent-hours/week until the IT fix lands April 25
• Estimated savings: **$2,000–$2,500 in agent cost per week**

**Total exposure before April 25:** ~$9,500 in agent time + member retention risk. The coaching script note already deployed is the highest-ROI action available before the IT fix.
suggested_chips: Have Finance refresh the Q1 cost model
When is the IT fix expected?

confidence.rationale: Cost model uses partial Q1 data — fully-loaded $28/hour figure has not been refreshed since January. Member-attrition coefficient (23%) is from a single retrospective study and may not reflect the current member base. Estimates have ±18% range.

nfcu_wf_cost_impact_resolved.ai_message:
**Re-modeled with refreshed Q1 inputs.** Finance supplied the closed Q1 fully-loaded cost ($30.40/hr, up from $28) and the attrition coefficient was re-estimated against the FY26 member base (now 19%, down from 23%).

**Updated picture:**
• Agent capacity wasted this week: **$5,472** (was $5,040)
• Members at elevated churn risk: **65** (was 78)
• Total exposure before April 25: **~$11,000** (was $9,500)

The coaching script note remains the highest-ROI action. Confidence is now 84% because both inputs are anchored to validated FY26 data rather than a partial Q1 snapshot.

confidence.rationale: Cost inputs now anchored to closed Q1 data and FY26 attrition cohort. Model variance reduced to ±6%.
